mode_contract.static: the social client is absent; shared mutations are never simulated
mode_contract.blob: account, profile, post, reply, follow, like, discover, and following feed remain available; relational community, edit, governance, moderation, appeal, and viewer-control requests fail visibly as unavailable with no optimistic success
mode_contract.postgres: the complete repository-defined social authority is reachable through actor-specific, exact-method consumer controls

archive_separation: Public edit, removal, moderation, reports, appeals, membership, and viewer controls operate only on server authority. They never delete or rewrite a private archive record or local profile.
consumer_journey: A signed-in person can open or create a community, join or leave, read its authoritative feed, publish, open a dedicated thread, edit their public post, report another post, mute or block an account, fork a community, submit an appeal, and—only when the returned community membership proves moderator or owner authority—moderate or decide an appeal. Owner-only role controls are absent for every other actor.
release_rule: A capability is a product feature only when the consumer reaches its exact operation and HTTP method, hides dangerous authority from unauthorized actors, refreshes authoritative projections after mutation, and reports unavailable modes without simulating shared success.
